articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has recognized Sahamati as the Self-Regulatory Organisation for the Account Aggregator ecosystem. This move formalizes governance and operational standards for financial data sharing. Sahamati, a not-for-profit entity, will now oversee this crucial network. The ecosystem facilitates consent-based sharing of financial data, benefiting credit underwriting and customer assessment.

articles.title: The Structural Hole In India's Data Protection Law
articles.summary: In the year 2023, when India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act was passed, it was welcomed as a long overdue development in India's data protection framework. After nearly a decade of failed legislative efforts, a comprehensive data protection statute had finally materialized. The act begins with a language suggesting consent centric model of data governance: personal data may only be processed with the free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous consent of the data principal.

After nearly a decade of failed legislative efforts, a comprehensive data protection statute had finally materialized. articles.title: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, boosts rupee with new measures
articles.summary: The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee unanimously held the repo rate at 5.25% and maintained a neutral stance, citing amplified inflation risks. To bolster the rupee, the central bank introduced multiple measures, including expanding accessible securities for foreign investors and increasing investment limits for NRIs. These actions aim to attract foreign capital and strengthen India's balance of payments.

articles.title: Rate of interest on Government of India Floating Rate Bond 2031
articles.summary: The rate of interest on Government of India Floating Rate Bond 2031 (FRB 2031) applicable for the half year June 07, 2026 to December 06, 2026 shall be 6.64 per cent per annum. It may be recalled that FRB 2031 carries a coupon, which has a base rate equivalent to the average of the Weighted Average Yield of last three auctions (from the rate fixing day i.e. June 07, 2026) of 182 Day T-Bills, plus a fixed spread of One per cent. Ajit Prasad            Deputy General Manager (Communications)     P

articles.title: Plea In Supreme Court Challenges Re-Appointment Of Deepak Prakash As Bihar Minister Without Membership Of Legislature
articles.summary: A writ petition has been filed in the Supreme Court challenging the re-appointment of Deepak Prakash as Bihar's Panchayati Raj Minister. The plea argues that Prakash, not being a member of the state legislature, violates constitutional provisions (Article 164(4)), which allow non-legislators to serve as ministers for only six months unless they secure legislative membership. The Supreme Court is now tasked with adjudicating this constitutional matter.

The plea states that Prakash is not a member of either House of the State legislature and therefore can't take any post in the State Government's ministry. It has been stated that, as per Article 164(4) of the Constitution, a non-legislator can remain a minister for six consecutive months, during which he must secure the membership of the State legislature. articles.summary: The Odisha government has increased reservation quotas for Scheduled Tribes (STs) from 12% to 22.5% and for Scheduled Castes (SCs) from 8% to 16.25% in medical and technical education courses. The regulator has sought explanations from UPPCL regarding the inclusion of these old dues in the surcharge.

articles.summary: The price of a 14.2-kg domestic LPG cylinder has been increased by Rs 29, following a previous hike of Rs 60 on March 7. Industry sources noted that the hike partially offset losses from domestic LPG sales.

As compliance requirements tighten globally, manufacturers are reassessing how efficiency, wastewater management and competitiveness intersect.

articles.title: Specific Relief Act | Buyer's Delay In Issuing Legal Notice To Seller Not Ground To Deny Specific Performance : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a buyer's delay in issuing a legal notice to a seller does not justify denying specific performance of an agreement to sell, provided the suit is filed within the limitation period. The Court held that such delays are irrelevant to the relief sought. This ruling clarifies the legal position on specific performance under the Specific Relief Act, ensuring fairness in contract enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that when a suit seeking specific performance of an agreement to sell is filed within the limitation, any delay in issuing a legal notice is irrelevant to the denial of relief.

articles.title: Rare Caracal Sighted In Kuno National Park, 4th Endangered Species Reappear In Wildlife Sanctuary After Introduction Of Cheetahs
articles.summary: A rare caracal, a wild cat listed under Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972, was spotted in Kuno National Park for the first time in decades. The sighting was confirmed by Uttam Kumar Sharma, Field Director of Project Cheetah, who stated it occurred last month. This marks the fourth endangered species to reappear in the sanctuary following the introduction of cheetahs. The event underscores the park's ecological significance and potential conservation policy needs.

articles.title: Greece to tax gains from crypto, sources say
articles.summary: Greece is set to introduce a 15% capital gains tax on cryptocurrencies. The Finance Ministry is drafting a law for parliamentary submission. The draft invites public feedback before finalization. This marks a proactive step by the judiciary to integrate AI while ensuring accountability in legal processes.

articles.title: Trinamool dissolves all West Bengal committees; to undergo introspection, performance review
articles.summary: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) in West Bengal has dissolved all party committees and announced an introspection and performance review amid rising internal tensions. The move suggests efforts to address organizational weaknesses and potential governance challenges ahead.

articles.summary: Fino Payments Bank has partnered with Ezee.ai to implement an AI-enabled Loan Origination System, Business Rules Engine, and Collections Management Platform. The deployment of AI tools highlights the growing integration of artificial intelligence in India's banking and fintech sectors.

This proposal is part of a broader review of EU trade defenses, including faster anti-dumping and anti-subsidy investigations and potential measures to address overcapacity. The review is expected by the third quarter. These steps could reshape global trade dynamics, affecting countries like India that are part of international supply networks.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that national sovereignty takes precedence over personal liberty in cases involving narcotic drugs and psychotropic substances. This ruling was made in the context of a drugs case and underscores the judiciary's stance on balancing individual rights with national security concerns.

articles.summary: India's Index of Industrial Production (IIP) growth slowed to 4.9%, with a new base year (2022-23) and an updated basket of 1,042 products. The data highlights a dip in mining activity, raising questions about industrial health and potential economic policy adjustments.

articles.title: Why PhysicsWallah is taking a U-turn on student lending
articles.summary: PhysicsWallah has reversed its decision to directly lend to students through its subsidiary FinZ Finance. Instead, it will now collaborate with third-party non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) for student financing. The move reflects a strategic shift in how edtech firms approach student loans in India's education sector. This could impact access to education financing and prompt discussions on regulatory oversight for such lending practices.

The TMC has dissolved all legislative panels in response. The dispute involves the anti-defection law, which requires two-thirds support (54 MLAs) to avoid disqualification for a breakaway faction.
articles.raw_summary: Under the anti-defection law, a breakaway faction requires the support of at least two-thirds of a legislature party to avoid disqualification. With the TMC having 80 MLAs in the Assembly, the threshold stands at 54.

articles.title: The move into gold by other nations has happened before. It’s bad news for US dollar
articles.summary: Central banks worldwide are reducing their holdings of U.S. assets in favor of gold, a trend last seen in 1971 when nations moved away from dollars before the U.S. suspended gold convertibility. The shift reflects concerns about the dollar's stability and could impact global financial systems. For India, this may influence foreign exchange reserves, trade strategies, and economic policies as the country navigates a changing monetary landscape.
articles.raw_summary: The recent shift from U.S. assets to gold mirrors 1971, when central banks dumped dollars before President Nixon ended gold convertibility.

This framework aims to enhance efficiency while upholding judicial authority and due process.

articles.title: A murder in the UK reopens debate on whether Sikhs should be allowed to carry kirpans in public
articles.summary: A recent murder in the UK has reignited discussions about whether Sikhs should be permitted to carry kirpans—ceremonial daggers—in public. The debate centers on Sikh tradition, which links the kirpan to the duty to protect others, but also acknowledges that this does not grant a legal right to use force. The incident may prompt broader conversations about religious freedoms and public safety laws.

articles.title: In A First, Five Women Lawyers Elected To Bar Council Of Maharashtra & Goa
articles.summary: For the first time, five women advocates have been elected to the Bar Council of Maharashtra & Goa (BCMG), alongside 18 male advocates. The elected women are Ranjana Bhosale, Kshitija Wadatkar, Anita Castellino, Madhavi Potdar, and Uma Bhatta. This marks a significant step in gender representation within legal governance bodies. The elections were conducted through a complex preferential voting system, with Advocate Parijat Pandey securing the highest votes.

Kshitija Wadatkar, 3. Anita Castellino, 4. Madhavi Potdar and 5. Uma Bhatta

It provides guidance on tax rates, applicable ITR forms, and compliance requirements under India's income tax laws. The focus is on ensuring accurate reporting to avoid penalties.

articles.title: Mutual fund houses restrict investments into gold ETFs, FoFs
articles.summary: Leading mutual fund houses, including HDFC, ICICI Prudential, and Nippon India, are temporarily restricting large lump-sum investments in gold ETFs and fund of funds. This move aligns with the government's policy to discourage gold purchases, following an increase in import duty. The restrictions primarily target large investors, with exceptions for authorized participants and market makers, while SIPs remain largely unaffected.

The restrictions primarily target large investors, with exceptions for authorized participants and market makers, while SIPs remain largely unaffected.

articles.title: Bangladesh FM Khalilur Rahman elected president of 81st UNGA, Jaishankar congratulates counterpart
articles.summary: Bangladesh's Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman has been elected President of the 81st United Nations General Assembly (UNGA). Rahman, who took office as Bangladesh's foreign minister earlier this year, is the first Bangladeshi to hold this position since 1986-87. External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar congratulated Rahman on his election. The role may influence international diplomacy and regional dynamics, including India's engagements with the UN and Bangladesh.
articles.raw_summary: Bangladesh last had presidency of UNGA in the 41st (1986-87) session. Rahman was sworn in as Bangladesh’s foreign minister in February this year.

articles.title: E85 fuel to get Rs 20/litre cheaper as India ramps up ethanol push
articles.summary: India is launching E85 petrol, an 85% ethanol blend, at discounted prices. This move aims to boost clean fuel adoption and cut reliance on imported fossil fuels. The government plans a phased rollout of E85 dispensing stations nationwide. Vehicle manufacturers are introducing E85 compatible models. This initiative is expected to benefit farmers and save foreign exchange.

The government plans a phased rollout of E85 dispensing stations nationwide. Vehicle manufacturers are introducing E85 compatible models. This initiative is expected to benefit farmers and save foreign exchange.

articles.title: Oil prices fall on mounting hopes for de-escalation in US-Iran War
articles.summary: Oil prices fell on Friday as traders reduced expectations of a US-Iran war escalation. Brent and WTI crude futures were set for their first weekly gains in three weeks due to earlier Middle East tensions and limited Strait of Hormuz traffic, but gains were limited by unexpected inventory levels and falling demand. The US and Iran are the key parties involved in the geopolitical context affecting oil markets.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices declined Friday as traders perceived a reduced likelihood of renewed conflict between the US and Iran. Despite this, both Brent and WTI crude futures were poised for their first weekly gains in three weeks, influenced by earlier Middle East tensions and limited Strait of Hormuz traffic. However, gains were tempered by unexpected inventory levels and falling demand.

articles.title: Odisha doubles rice milling charges, to encourage paddy procurement
articles.summary: The Odisha government has doubled the charges for milling rice to encourage paddy procurement from farmers and support millers. Parboiled rice milling charges increased from Rs 20 to Rs 40 per quintal, while raw rice charges rose from Rs 10 to Rs 20 per quintal. The decision aims to strengthen the state's paddy processing sector and is effective immediately.
articles.raw_summary: The Odisha government has doubled rice milling charges. This move aims to boost paddy procurement from farmers and support millers. Charges for parboiled rice increased from Rs 20 to Rs 40 per quintal. Raw rice milling charges rose from Rs 10 to Rs 20 per quintal. This decision is effective immediately. It is expected to strengthen the state's paddy processing.

articles.title: A movement losing momentum and daughters as Haryana’s sex ratio declines
articles.summary: Launched by PM Modi in Panipat in 2015, the Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao campaign initially delivered strong results. Haryana’s Sex Ratio at Birth rose from 838 in 2010 to 923 by 2019, driven by strict raids on pre-natal testing centres, inter-State crackdowns, monthly video conference reviews, and fixed accountability. However, data for the first four months of 2026 show that the number of girl babies born has dipped below 900, sparking fears that the programme is losing focus and momentum, reports

articles.title: Govt to replace WPI with Producer Price Index, new series from June 15
articles.summary: The Indian government will replace the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) with the Producer Price Index (PPI) starting June 15. This change aims to better measure price changes at the producer level. The WPI is currently used to track average price changes in a basket of commodities. The shift to PPI reflects an update in economic indicators used for policy and analysis.
articles.raw_summary: WPI is a key indicator for measuring the average change in the prices of a fixed basket of commodities at the initial stage of a commercial transaction over a given period, with reference to a base year.

articles.title: LPG consumption declined 19% in May, petrol-diesel increased
articles.summary: India's diesel and petrol consumption increased by 0.8% and 2.8% respectively in May compared to last year, while LPG usage declined by 19%. The shift in fuel consumption patterns could reflect changing energy demand or economic activity. The data, sourced from The Hindu Economy, highlights trends that may influence future energy policies or regulatory measures.
articles.raw_summary: Consumption of diesel and petrol in May rose 0.8% and 2.8% respectively from the comparable period last year.

articles.title: Unseasonal snowfall blocks the path for migratory pastoralists
articles.summary: A Gujjar family from Rajouri district in Jammu and Kashmir was traveling with their livestock to high-altitude pastures when fresh snowfall blocked their path at Peer Ki Gali. Authorities suspended traffic on the Mughal Road, halting their journey. The incident highlights challenges faced by migratory pastoralists due to extreme weather, raising concerns about infrastructure and climate resilience in the region.

articles.title: After eight lions die from Babesia infection, treatment and isolation controls spread
articles.summary: Eight Asiatic lions, including cubs, died from a Babesia parasite infection in Gujarat, while 17 others are being treated. The disease spreads via tick bites and causes weakness and anemia. State forest and veterinary departments are managing the outbreak within a 10-kilometer radius. The incident may prompt policy responses to control wildlife diseases and protect endangered species.

articles.title: A bauxite mining project, contested consent and growing tensions
articles.summary: In April 2026, over 200 police personnel forcibly entered Kantamal village in Odisha’s Rayagada and Kalahandi districts, according to locals. The village is part of the Sijimali range, where a bauxite mining project is contested by residents. The incident highlights growing tensions over land rights and environmental concerns. Activists and villagers allege lack of proper consent and demand policy intervention.

Kantamal is one of over 44 villages nestled in the foothills of Sijimali range in Odisha s Rayagada and Kalahandi districts. More than 200 police personnel had encircled the village, say the residents. [ ]

articles.title: Meta’s AI smart glasses are one switch away from recognising and naming faces
articles.summary: A security researcher found that Meta's Ray-Ban smart glasses companion app contains a fully developed facial recognition system, though the feature is currently disabled. The discovery highlights concerns about AI-powered surveillance and data privacy, especially as such technologies become more widespread. Meta has not activated the feature, but its existence raises questions about future policy and regulatory oversight in India.

The controversy raises concerns about the reliability and security of digital assessment methods in India's education system, potentially prompting policy reviews or reforms.

The TMC was founded by Banerjee in 1998 after separating from the Congress and has since been a dominant force in West Bengal. articles.title: Economists see limited downside to FY27 growth despite oil and geopolitical risks
articles.summary: A panel of economists, including Aditi Nayar (ICRA), Sakshi Gupta (HDFC Bank), and Rajani Sinha (CareEdge Ratings), stated that India's strong services sector, healthy consumption, and rising investment could mitigate risks to growth in FY27 despite challenges like oil price volatility and geopolitical tensions. The economists highlighted these factors as buffers against external shocks.
articles.raw_summary: A panel of economists comprising Aditi Nayar, Chief Economist at ICRA, Sakshi Gupta, Vice President and Senior Economist at HDFC Bank, and Rajani Sinha, Chief Economist at CareEdge Ratings, said the robust performance of the services sector, healthy consumption and stronger investment activity have provided a cushion against external shocks and could help limit the downside risks to growth in FY27.

articles.title: ATF for international airlines cut 27%; commercial LPG, 5-kg cylinder prices raised
articles.summary: Domestic airlines will continue paying ₹104,927.18 per kilolitre for Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF), a rate set on April 1 that only partially addressed the required increase. Meanwhile, the price of commercial 5-kg LPG cylinders has been raised. The unchanged ATF pricing since April suggests ongoing regulatory or policy challenges affecting fuel costs for airlines and consumers.
articles.raw_summary: Domestic carriers will continue to pay ₹104,927.18 per kilolitre for ATF, the rate that became applicable on April 1, when only a fourth of the required increase was passed on to airlines. The prices have remained unchanged since then.

articles.title: OECD cuts global growth forecast, warns prolonged US-Iran war could trigger recessions
articles.summary: The OECD reduced its global growth forecast, warning that a prolonged US-Iran war in West Asia could disrupt shipping and damage energy infrastructure, slowing global growth to 2.1% in 2026 and 1.8% in 2027. The organization cautioned that such disruptions could have severe consequences, including potential recessions. The scenario assumes continued conflicts into 2027, affecting energy supplies and trade routes.
articles.raw_summary: OECD cautioned that a prolonged West Asia conflict would have far more severe consequences. Under a scenario where shipping disruptions and damage to energy infrastructure continue into 2027, global growth could slow to 2.1% in 2026 and 1.8% in 2027.

articles.title: HC junks contempt plea, upholds right of press to report on court order before it’s signed by judge
articles.summary: A division bench of the High Court dismissed a contempt plea against three newspapers that reported on an oral court order before it was formally signed by the judge. The single-judge bench had previously initiated contempt proceedings against the newspapers for reporting the oral order. The division bench upheld the press's right to report on court proceedings, reinforcing media freedom and the public's right to information.
articles.raw_summary: Three newspapers got clean chit from division bench after single-judge bench took exception to reporting of oral court order   initiated contempt proceedings.

articles.summary: A report by NBC News suggests a Chinese missile may have been responsible for downing a US F-15E fighter jet in Iran. The report also claims China likely provided Iran with an early warning radar system during the early stages of the West Asia conflict. The growing military and diplomatic ties between Beijing and Tehran raise concerns about regional stability and India's strategic interests.
articles.raw_summary: China, according to the NBC News report, also likely provided Iran with an early warning radar system during the early days of war in West Asia.

The situation may prompt discussions on aviation sector support or trade-related measures to mitigate such disruptions in the future.

Foreign nationals accounted for 17 of the 21 fatalities. Prime Mini

articles.title: Saved by the barrel: Why crude hasn't hit the $200 mark
articles.summary: Crude oil prices have not reached the feared $200 per barrel mark despite disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil transit route. The resilience in prices is attributed to increased US oil exports, weaker demand from China, and the use of alternative shipping routes. While some oil continues to flow through the strait, overall transits have declined. Temporary measures and subdued global demand have prevented a worst-case scenario, but market stability remains dependent on short-term
articles.raw_summary: Despite fears of $200 oil following Strait of Hormuz disruptions, prices remain below $100. This resilience is attributed to increased US exports, weaker Chinese demand, and alternative supply routes. While some shipping through the strait continues, overall transits are down. Emergency measures and subdued demand have averted the worst-case scenario, but market stability hinges on temporary solutions.

articles.title: Weather Bee: What does onset of monsoon in Kerala mean for seasonal rains?
